title: Two Russian men jailed in Angola for terrorism and spying
description:
The men were convicted over alleged plan to stage coup and organise deadly demonstrations over fuel prices last year

Two Russian men have been found guilty by an Angolan court of terrorism and spying after being accused of planning a coup and helping to organise deadly demonstrations in the southern African country last year.

Lev Lakshtanov and Igor Ratchin were sentenced to eight and 11 years in jail respectively. They had denied the charges, claiming they were in Angola for cultural and business activities.

Continue reading...
summary: Two Russian men, Lev Lakshtanov and Igor Ratchin, have been convicted by an Angolan court of terrorism and spying, and sentenced to 8 and 11 years in jail respectively, for allegedly planning a coup and organizing deadly demonstrations in Angola last year. They had denied the charges, claiming they were in the country for cultural and business activities. The men were found guilty of planning a coup and helping to organize the demonstrations over fuel prices.

title: MoD cancels army training in Kenya over powers to prosecute British soldiers
description:
September exercise called off after Kenya raises concerns over funding and jurisdiction for criminal offences

The British army has been forced to cancel training for its troops in Kenya after the Kenyan government refused to ratify a bilateral defence agreement.

While the UK government wanted to renew the existing agreement, Kenyan lawmakers had sought changes to give local authorities greater powers to hold British soldiers accountable for any offences committed in the country.

Continue reading...
summary: The British army has cancelled its army training in Kenya due to a dispute over a bilateral defence agreement. The Kenyan government refused to ratify the agreement, seeking changes to give local authorities more power to prosecute British soldiers for crimes committed in the country. The UK government wanted to renew the existing agreement.

title: Health worker monitored for Ebola in London hospital after working in DRC
description:
UK resident was evacuated from Democratic Republic of the Congo where there is an outbreak of the disease

A humanitarian worker is being monitored at a specialist hospital in London after potential exposure to the Ebola virus.

The UK resident was medically evacuated from the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) where the worker had been treating patients with the disease, the UK Health Security Agency (UKHSA) said.

Continue reading...
summary: A UK resident who worked in the Democratic Republic of the Congo is being monitored for Ebola at a London hospital after being medically evacuated due to potential exposure to the virus. The individual had been treating patients with the disease. The UK Health Security Agency is overseeing the situation.

title: Healthy diet too expensive for one in three people globally, UN report finds
description:
Global hunger declined for the third year in a row in 2025 but experts call for action to bring down costs of fruit, vegetables and dairy as report shows 2.69bn people can’t afford to eat well

One in three people cannot afford to eat a healthy diet, a new report has found, prompting calls for urgent action on food affordability.

There are 2.69 billion people unable to afford a diverse diet that meets their energy and nutrient needs, and it will take “immense” amounts of work to achieve the global target of zero hunger by 2030, the annual report from five UN agencies warned.

Continue reading...
summary: A new UN report found that one in three people globally cannot afford a healthy diet, with 2.69 billion people unable to afford a diverse diet that meets their energy and nutrient needs. This highlights the need for urgent action on food affordability to achieve the global target of zero hunger by 2030. Global hunger declined for the third year in a row in 2025, but experts still call for action.

title: São Tomé and Príncipe heads to polls in tense presidential election
description:
Incumbent Carlos Vila Nova hopes to defeat his former party and secure second term as independent

Voters in São Tomé and Príncipe go to the polls for a presidential election on Sunday as one of Africa’s least populous countries seeks to burnish its democratic credentials.

According to the National Election Commission, about 142,000 people are registered to vote in the tiny African state’s elections, approximately 15% of whom live in the diaspora.

Continue reading...
summary: Voters in São Tomé and Príncipe are heading to the polls for a presidential election, with incumbent Carlos Vila Nova seeking a second term as an independent candidate. The election aims to showcase the country's democratic credentials. Approximately 142,000 people are registered to vote, with 15% living abroad.

title: Iran War and Trump’s Tariffs Threaten a Resilient U.S. Economy
description: As renewed fighting with Iran sends oil and gas prices soaring, President Trump has imposed new global tariffs that could push up prices further.
summary: The US economy faces potential threats from renewed fighting with Iran and President Trump's new global tariffs, which could lead to increased oil and gas prices. The situation has the potential to impact the economy despite its current resilience.

title: Trump’s Global Tariffs: What’s the Latest on the Trade War
description: The president has unveiled a complicated system to forge ahead with his trade war, after the Supreme Court struck down his original levies.
summary: US President Trump has introduced a new system to implement his trade war, following a Supreme Court ruling that struck down his original tariffs. The move aims to continue the trade war, but the details of the new system are complex. The development is part of a broader trade dispute.

title: Trump Administration Imposed New Tariffs of Around 10% on Over 80 Nations
description: The administration launched new duties tied to countries’ imports of goods made with forced labor, replacing a 10 percent tariff immediately after it expired.
summary: The Trump administration has imposed new tariffs of around 10% on over 80 nations, targeting countries that import goods made with forced labor. This move replaces a previous 10% tariff that had expired. The new duties aim to address concerns over labor practices in these countries.

title: Brent Crude Oil Prices Top $100 as Conflict With Iran Drags On
description: Stocks in Asia were down following a sell-off in the U.S. markets over soaring oil prices, rising borrowing costs and worries about the technology industry.
summary: Brent crude oil prices have surpassed $100, causing a sell-off in U.S. markets and affecting Asian stocks. The increase is attributed to the ongoing conflict with Iran. This has also led to concerns about rising borrowing costs and the technology industry.

title: The Cost of Trump’s New Tariffs, and the $100 Million Popcorn Bucket Market
description: Plus, the Friday news quiz.
summary: The article appears to be related to the business sector, specifically discussing the impact of Trump's new tariffs and the market for a $100 million popcorn bucket. However, the provided information is limited, and the article seems to be more of an introduction or teaser rather than a comprehensive news piece.

title: Southport killer Axel Rudakubana appears to be manipulating the system, families' lawyer says
description: On Thursday, Axel Rudakubana was moved from prison to a secure psychiatric hospital in Berkshire.
summary: Axel Rudakubana, a convicted killer, has been transferred from prison to a secure psychiatric hospital, prompting concerns from his families' lawyer that he may be manipulating the system. The lawyer believes Rudakubana is taking advantage of the situation.

title: Tens of thousands evacuated from French tourist spot and near Madrid as wildfires spread
description: The fire has raged in the region for several days with officials now ordering the evacuation of the entire Cap Ferret peninsula.
summary: Tens of thousands of people have been evacuated from a French tourist spot and a nearby area in Madrid due to spreading wildfires, with the fire having raged in the region for several days. The evacuation order was issued for the entire Cap Ferret peninsula. The situation is ongoing.

title: 'Extremely significant' bomb in car intercepted on way to NI, Irish police say
description: Two people are arrested after a vehicle was stopped in County Monaghan in an operation targeting dissident republican activity.
summary: Irish police have intercepted a car carrying a bomb on its way to Northern Ireland, arresting two people in the process. The operation targeted dissident republican activity. The incident highlights ongoing security concerns in the region.

title: Angela Rayner rules out rent controls in England
description: The housing secretary tells the BBC that the Renters Rights' Act is already having a significant impact on the market.
summary: Housing Secretary Angela Rayner has ruled out implementing rent controls in England, citing the impact of the existing Renters Rights' Act on the market. The Act aims to improve the rights of renters, and Rayner believes it is already having a significant effect.

title: Chris Mason: What his first week in power tells us about Andy Burnham
description: The fledgling Burnham administration has been seeking to find its feet and make a decent first impression at the same time, the BBC's political editor Chris Mason explains.
summary: The Burnham administration is in its early stages, and BBC's political editor Chris Mason provides insight into what the first week in power has shown. The article discusses the administration's efforts to make a good impression and find its footing.

title: Spain declares national emergency over wildfires
description: At least 10,000 people have been evacuated following wildfires in the central region of Madrid.
summary: Spain has declared a national emergency due to widespread wildfires in the central region of Madrid, resulting in the evacuation of at least 10,000 people. The situation is ongoing, with efforts focused on containing the fires and protecting residents. The exact cause of the wildfires is not yet known.

title: Spain’s De la Fuente slams Argentina’s World Cup conduct as ‘unacceptable’
description: While Argentina faced criticism through World Cup 2026, post-final altercations are now being investigated.
summary: Argentina is facing criticism and investigation for their conduct after the World Cup, with Spain's De la Fuente labeling it as 'unacceptable'. The controversy follows the team's performance in the 2026 World Cup.

title: At least 11 killed in Ukraine as Moscow and Kyiv continue to trade attacks
description: Six people also killed in Ukrainian attack on Russia’s Kirov, and Wildberries warehouses hit again.
summary: At least 11 people have been killed in Ukraine due to ongoing attacks between Moscow and Kyiv, with additional casualties reported in a Ukrainian attack on Russia's Kirov. The conflict continues to escalate with further strikes on key targets, including Wildberries warehouses. The situation remains volatile in the region.

title: Australia World Cup star Volpato faces speeding, cocaine charges
description: Cristian Volpato, who switched from Italy to Australia before World Cup 2026, failed a roadside drug test in Sydney.
summary: Australian World Cup star Cristian Volpato is facing charges of speeding and cocaine possession after a roadside drug test in Sydney. Volpato recently switched his national allegiance from Italy to Australia. The incident has raised concerns about his behavior.

title: Is the US-Saudi nuclear deal on, or in limbo?
description: Trump's last-minute demand for Saudi normalisation with Israel has complicated the already sensitive deal.
summary: The US-Saudi nuclear deal is in jeopardy due to a last-minute demand from former US President Trump for Saudi normalization with Israel, adding complexity to the already sensitive agreement.

title: Trump-backed SAVE Act is newest effort to collect private voter information
description: The administration is trying to consolidate state voter data into federal hands.
summary: The US administration is attempting to collect private voter information through a new bill, the SAVE Act, which has the backing of former President Trump. This move aims to consolidate state voter data into federal hands. The bill's intentions and potential implications are currently unclear.

title: LIVE:  ABC News Live
description: 24/7 coverage of breaking news and live events
summary: This article provides 24/7 coverage of breaking news and live events, indicating a global focus on current events.

title: Multiple injured, 1 critically, after seaplane with 11 aboard crashes in Wash. state
description: Multiple people were injured, including one critically, after a seaplane with 11 people aboard, crashed off the coast of Washington state, according to authorities.
summary: A seaplane carrying 11 people crashed off the coast of Washington state, resulting in multiple injuries, including one critical. The incident is currently under investigation by authorities.

title: Trump set to impose sweeping tariffs on 60 trade partners
description: The tariff closely resembles a proposal issued in June.
summary: US President Trump plans to impose sweeping tariffs on 60 trade partners, mirroring a proposal from June, in a move that could have significant economic implications. The tariffs are expected to affect a wide range of countries and industries. The exact details of the plan are not yet clear.

title: WATCH:  Video shows seaplane crash off Washington state
description: Dramatic video captured the moment a tourist floatplane carrying 11 people crashed off the Washington coast near Seattle. Everyone on board survived.
summary: A tourist floatplane carrying 11 people crashed off the Washington coast near Seattle, but all on board survived. The incident was captured on dramatic video. The crash occurred near Seattle.
